subject: Added catching error when calculating elapsedTime.
commit_message:
Added catching error when calculating elapsedTime.

Issue-ID: LOG-1148
Change-Id: I8f94b066e1841322199c95f7de82c1a4e76fcd2f
Signed-off-by: Brittany Plummer (bp896r) <bp896r@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/logging-analytics-master-verify-java/280/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/logging-analytics-master-verify-java/280

Patch Set 1:

If we can not determine the elapsed time, setting a default time feels misleading to me. Is anyone opposed to not setting this value in instances in which the value cannot be determined?

Also is this currently happening during testing? Do we have any root cause?

Patch Set 1:

I understand that setting a default elapsed time can be confusing. The initial logic behind this was to keep it so that the log line would still be valid if elapsedTime could not be calculated. I am seeing it where entryTimestamp is null so a NullPointerException is thrown. However, since the entry or invoke timestamps will also be null in that case, that would make the log lines invalid anyway. I can remove setting the default elapsedTime, but I think that we should still catch exceptions and print a warning to identify that we weren't able to set elapsedTime

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I understand that setting a default elapsed time can be confusing. The initial logic behind this was to keep it so that the log line would still be valid if elapsedTime could not be calculated. I am seeing it where entryTimestamp is null so a NullPointerException is thrown. However, since the entry or invoke timestamps will also be null in that case, that would make the log lines invalid anyway. I can remove setting the default elapsedTime, but I think that we should still catch exceptions and print a warning to identify that we weren't able to set elapsedTime

I agree that it should be a "best effort" service meaning log as best as possible instead of crashing out. If we log the exception or error message will someone be able to debug and fix it?

Patch Set 1:

>

> > 
> > I understand that setting a default elapsed time can be confusing. The initial logic behind this was to keep it so that the log line would still be valid if elapsedTime could not be calculated. I am seeing it where entryTimestamp is null so a NullPointerException is thrown. However, since the entry or invoke timestamps will also be null in that case, that would make the log lines invalid anyway. I can remove setting the default elapsedTime, but I think that we should still catch exceptions and print a warning to identify that we weren't able to set elapsedTime
> 
> I agree that it should be a "best effort" service meaning log as best as possible instead of crashing out. If we log the exception or error message will someone be able to debug and fix it?

I think it should be okay. With the way it is printing now with the NPE and the whole stack trace from AbstractAuditLogFilter, it is being confused for an execution stopping error. I think logging the warning and exception message will prevent some confusion here, while also providing some insight into the issue.
